title: Barista Onboarding: First Shift
description: SOP for a new barista on day one. Covers shift opening, basic drink preparation, and guest interaction.

steps.title: Workstation preparation
steps.content: Arrive 15 minutes before shift start. Put on a clean uniform and apron. Wash hands (40 seconds with soap). Check the bar: surface cleanliness, milk, beans, cups, lids, stirrers stock. Turn on the espresso machine — 20-minute warm-up. Start the display fridge and verify temperature (+2…+6°C / 36-43°F).
steps.key_takeaway: Your shift starts 15 minutes before the first guest, not at opening time.

steps.title: Grind calibration and espresso dial-in
steps.content: Pull a test shot: 18g coffee in, 36g out, in 25-30 seconds. Faster? Grind finer. Slower? Grind coarser. Taste: balanced, no sharp acidity or bitterness. Repeat until you hit the standard. Calibration is the foundation of the whole shift.
steps.key_takeaway: 18g → 36g → 25-30 sec. This is a formula, not a rough guide.

steps.title: Cappuccino preparation
steps.content: 150 ml cold milk into the pitcher. Steam to 60-65°C (140-149°F), glossy texture, no large bubbles. Pull espresso into a 180 ml cup. Pour milk from 3-5 cm above, then lower the pitcher for the pattern. Serve immediately — cappuccino lives 90 seconds.
steps.key_takeaway: 65°C is the ceiling. Above that, milk is scalded and flavor is gone.

steps.title: Greeting and taking the order
steps.content: Eye contact + smile within 5 seconds of the guest appearing. Greeting: 'Hi, what can I get for you?'. If the guest hesitates — offer two options, not five. Repeat the order aloud before ringing it up. State the total clearly. Hand over receipt and order number.
steps.key_takeaway: Two options, not five. Less choice = faster decision.

steps.title: Cashier and payment handling
steps.content: Ring each item separately — do not 'combine in your head'. Clarify payment method: card, cash, mobile. On change — state the amount aloud. If terminal fails — stay calm, politely offer an alternative. Never accept 'pay later' or 'on credit'.
steps.key_takeaway: Each item separately. Saving 5 seconds = mistake in the report.

steps.title: Handing the drink to the guest
steps.content: Call out the order: 'Cappuccino for Anna'. Place on the pickup counter with the handle facing the guest. Add a napkin and stirrer. Wish them a 'great day'. For takeaway — confirm the lid is sealed tight.
steps.key_takeaway: Name + eye contact on handoff. That's +20% in tips.

steps.title: Shift closing and cashout
steps.content: 30 minutes before closing: stop accepting orders for complex drinks. Backflush the espresso machine (3 cycles). Wipe all surfaces, wash pitchers. Pull the Z-report. Reconcile cash with the report — discrepancy over $1 = log it and notify the manager.
steps.key_takeaway: Don't hide discrepancies — log them. Honesty beats a clean report.

quiz.question: How many minutes before shift start should you arrive?
quiz.explanation: 15 minutes are needed for machine warm-up and bar check.

quiz.question: What is the correct espresso formula?
quiz.explanation: 18/36/25-30 is the international standard for classic espresso.

quiz.question: Maximum milk temperature for cappuccino?
quiz.explanation: Above 65°C milk protein denatures, flavor turns 'boiled'.

quiz.question: What to do with a cash discrepancy over $1?
options: Log it and notify the manager
quiz.explanation: Honest logging matters more than a 'clean' report. It's about trust.

quiz.question: How many drink options to offer a hesitating guest?
quiz.explanation: Two options speed up the decision. More creates choice paralysis.

manager_checklist: Barista arrived in uniform 15+ minutes before shift
Ran a test shot and calibrated the grind
Prepared cappuccino at correct milk temperature (spot-check with thermometer)
